basics.name: Byungwoo Yoo
basics.label: CTO / Software Engineer
basics.summary: Inspired by a professor's words, 'You should dedicate yourself to the country and its people because you received high-quality education at a lower cost,' I have been driven to make people's lives more convenient worldwide. As a core developer of LINE Messenger used by 600 million people globally, I have gained experience in launching a successful product. With my experience in entrepreneurship and exit in the US, as well as leading AI labs at top ed-tech companies in Korea, I am currently serving as CTO at Datarize.

work.name: Datarize
work.summary: A CRM SaaS platform for e-commerce operators
highlights: Established technical roadmaps and managed hiring and organization for BE, FE, Data, QA, and SDK chapters
Led the company's AX transformation and AI hackathon, resulting in the launch of three AI products
Designed enterprise-oriented APIs and handled technical sales

work.name: Mathpresso
work.position: VP of Engineering / AI Lab Lead
work.summary: A company operating the AI-based math problem-solving platform 'QANDA'
highlights: Led the development of AI-based digital textbooks used by major educational companies, resulting in the only profitable business unit (2023.02 – 2025.05)
Led multiple AI research projects (ML, search, calculator & DB) (2021.02 – 2023.02)
Redesigned search and ML services based on domain-driven design, reducing infrastructure costs by over 50%

work.name: Kasa Korea
work.summary: A company operating the blockchain-based real estate investment and trading platform 'Kasa'
highlights: Oversaw the development of various features for the API server and trading engine, leading to the successful launch of the service

work.name: Buzzvil
work.summary: A company operating the world's largest smartphone lock screen advertising platform 'BuzzScreen' (used by over 2 million people daily)
highlights: Led the development of ad and content allocation logic for BuzzScreen
Optimized search functionality, reducing infrastructure costs by 75% and providing faster response times
As a software architect, guided other engineers in building robust and sustainable microservices using Clean Architecture and DDD, ensuring zero downtime

work.name: Slidejoy
work.summary: A US-based startup acquired by Buzzvil
highlights: Reached break-even point and secured funding before being acquired (reported by TechCrunch)
Designed and developed the service using ad networks, and introduced Spring Boot for the backend

work.name: NAVER
highlights: Improved transmission rates (75% → 99.5%) as the lead developer of NNI (NHN Notification Infra), a core library for LINE, and gained experience in overseas business trips and on-site debugging
Developed and contributed to the Android version of the Netty network library as an open-source project
Introduced MVP software design to enhance team performance

work.name: InfoBank / DOSA
work.summary: Worked at InfoBank and DOSA as an alternative to military service
highlights: Participated in the development of m&Talk and Samsung ChatON

education.area: Department of Electrical Engineering (minor in Business Administration)

publications.name: Explain with Visual Keypoints Like a Real Mentor! A Benchmark for Multimodal Solution Explanation
publications.summary: Proposed a multimodal benchmark (MathExplain) for identifying and explaining visual keypoints in math problem-solving explanations. Co-authored.
